manufacturers.name: Notifier (Honeywell)
manufacturers.notes: Manufactured by Honeywell Limited, 7 Columbia Court, Norwest Business Park, Baulkham Hills NSW 2153. Inertia is a long-standing Australian conventional/addressable FIP brand under the Notifier umbrella.

product_families.name: Inertia
product_families.description: Notifier Inertia conventional FIP family — Australian-designed, AS 1603.4 / AS 1670 compliant. Includes IFS-2400, IFS-2600, IFS-008, INERTIA-2464/64, and the AFP-2800 analogue addressable variant.

models.name: Inertia 2400 (IFS-2400)
models.description: Notifier Inertia IFS-2400 conventional Fire Indicator Panel — 24-zone Australian-designed FIP supplied 2003–2005 by Honeywell Limited (ABN 74 000 646 882). Approved to AS 1603.4 (1987) under Australian SSL Approval No. 215. Subject to ACCC product recall PRA 2005/8072 (audible-warning fault on certain hardware revisions); replaced in many sites by the INERTIA-2464 and AFP-2800 platforms but still encountered in service across NSW commercial buildings.

standards.title: Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems — System design, installation and commissioning — Fire
standards.notes: Core FIP design/install anchor. Governs detector spacing, warning levels, FDCIE location, smoke-control interfaces, aspirating smoke detectors. Must know edition referenced on FSS for correct assessment. | lifecycle:current | corpus:referenced_directly_in_internal_docs | category:design_install_commissioning | relevance:FIP,VESDA,ancillary_control,smoke_control_interface,brigade_interface

standards.title: Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems — System design, installation and commissioning — Fire (2004 edition)
standards.notes: Older edition — useful for legacy system interpretation. References AS 1603.8, AS 1851.8, AS 4214, AS 4428 series, AS 7240. | lifecycle:superseded | corpus:found_in_drive | category:design_install_commissioning | relevance:FIP,VESDA,legacy_interpretation

standards.title: Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems — System design, installation and commissioning — Fire (1995 edition)
standards.notes: Section 6.3 Multipoint Aspirated Smoke Detectors — important for older VESDA-type design context. | lifecycle:superseded | corpus:found_in_drive | category:design_install_commissioning | relevance:FIP,VESDA,legacy_interpretation

standards.title: Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems (1986 edition)
standards.notes: Old legacy edition. Includes smoke detection sampling systems and commissioning/maintenance sections. Good for old panels and old site conditions. | lifecycle:superseded | corpus:found_in_drive | category:design_install_commissioning | relevance:FIP,VESDA,legacy_interpretation

standards.title: Emergency warning and intercom systems
standards.notes: Main modern EWIS design/install/commissioning standard. References AS 4428.16 and maintenance under AS 1851. | lifecycle:current | corpus:referenced_directly_in_internal_docs | category:ewis | relevance:EWIS,intercom,FIP_interface

standards.title: Emergency warning and intercom systems (2004 edition)
standards.notes: Legacy EWIS edition. Useful for older sites and equipment. | lifecycle:superseded | corpus:found_in_drive | category:ewis | relevance:EWIS,intercom,legacy_interpretation

standards.title: Routine service of fire protection systems and equipment
standards.notes: Primary routine service standard. Section 6: Fire detection and alarm systems. Appendix C: Baseline Data. Appendix D: Systems Interface Testing. Appendix F: Battery Capacity Testing. Appendix G: Fire Detector Testing. | lifecycle:current | corpus:referenced_directly_in_internal_docs | category:maintenance_routine_service | relevance:maintenance,testing,baseline_data,records,interface_testing,battery_testing

standards.title: Routine service of fire protection systems and equipment (2005 edition)
standards.notes: Amalgamated many older AS 1851 parts.
